rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 4
rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 4
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 4 Product: Laserfiche Forms 11 KB: 1014470 Published: July 11, 2023 Last Updated: December 20, 2023 Summary Laserfiche introduces various new features with Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 4. For a more detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1014469. Laserfiche Forms Server System Requirements CPU: 4-core or more CPU Memory: 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended) Operating system: Windows 10 Windows 11 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2016 Windows Server 2019 Windows Server 2022 .Net Framework 4.8 must be installed. Note: Hardware requirements may fluctuate based on the number of users logged in to the server. If you expect to have many simultaneous connections to your Laserfiche Forms server, we encourage you to configure it with a faster CPU and/or add more RAM. Note: Server operating systems (Windows Server 2019, etc.) are preferred. Database Server Microsoft SQL Server 2014 SP3 or above Microsoft SQL Server 2016 SP2 or above Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Microsoft SQL Server 2019 Laserfiche Forms Software Requirements Laserfiche Server Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Server (version 9 or later) or Laserfiche Directory Server. Forms is not compatible with standalone editions of Laserfiche (Executive, Desktop, etc.). In order to integrate with Laserfiche Audit trail with Advanced features and support more event classes and failure audit, Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Update 5 (or later) and Laserfiche Full Text Search 11.0 (or later). Web Server Internet Information Services (IIS): IIS 8.5 (Windows Server 2012 R2) IIS 10 (Windows 10, Windows 11, Windows Server 2016, Windows Server 2019, Windows Server 2022) ASP.NET 4.x must be enabled. Client Workstation Users can fill out forms that start a process in Chrome (latest version), Safari(Mac only), Firefox (latest version), Edge (Chromium based), Opera. The Laserfiche Forms Inbox, Form Designer, Process Designer, Administrative pages, etc. must be viewed in Edge (Chromium based), Firefox (latest version), or Chrome (latest version). Laserfiche Forms also supports mobile browsing (Chrome, Firefox, and Safari recommended). Known Issues Installation Issue: Update 4 may prevent forms with the Authorize.net payment gateway integration from properly loading. You may see the following error message when loading the form: "Laserfiche Forms has encountered a problem. An error occurred while retrieving your form. Please try resubmitting your request later. [LFF2105-FormLoadingError]." The update may improperly uninstall a component that is necessary for the integration. Run a repair of the installation to restore the missing component. (478928) Form designer: The row count of a collection/table will not be correct if the 'append sets' choice is enabled when a lookup rule returns less data than the previous selection and there is another field with a value inside the collection/table. (324476) Put the previous/next button for pagination under the payment/reCAPTCHA section (368245) Improve accessibility for date picker used when fill form (370821) Cannot open instance detail page if the instance has more than 10k worker instances (395346) Forms Team JS Filter variable declaration error when test the filter/run the filter multiple times (397025) Need a way to clear the field value submitted in previous step and the field is hidden by field rule in later steps (403080) Update "Manage Access" of report schedule will trigger new snapshot generation if scheduled time is in the past. (306739) When importing a form from a current version of Forms to a previous version of Forms, the import process may not import features present in the current version of Forms. Forms only checks a user's group membership when creating their session token upon signing in. This means that removing a user from a group may not be reflected in a change in a user's Forms security until after the user signs out and signs back in. (168505) You may receive an InvalidDataSent error when you submit a form after waiting more than thirty minutes, while signed in as a Directory Server user. (134714) In certain situations, Forms may not properly process a field rule to ignore a value when payment processing is turned on. (125410) Forms may not fully synchronize some users that are members of a Directory Server group when using Laserfiche Server authentication. (74678) When dragging a variable generated by a formula from one form to another form, the formula is not transferred to the second form. (128416) On an iPad, the signature dialog offers only the option to draw a signature. Typing a signature is not possible on an iPad. (145050) Custom JavaScript "window onload = function setDate() {" displays when previewing a form but does not display when the form is published. To work around this known issue, try modifying the line with "function setDate() {" and then move the function call into a document r

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Forms 11 Update 1 2 years ago https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014301/list-of-changes-for-forms-11-update-1 Forms 11 Summary This article details each change for Laserfiche Forms 11 update 1 (11.0 ... collec... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014301/list-of-changes-for-forms- ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Forms 11 Update 1
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Forms 11 Update 1
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Forms 11 Update 1 Product: Forms 11 KB: 1014301 Published: June 9, 2021 Last Updated: August 1, 2023 Summary This article details each change for Laserfiche Forms 11 update 1 (11.0.2106.10331). Updates In the new form designer, we have added localization support, enabled using lookup rules to fill fields inside of collections/tables as new sets/rows, support for offline forms with the Laserfiche App, session timeouts, backend validation, and the ability to convert classic forms to the new format. Additional new features include: Enhanced data source security to streamline the process of configuring lookup rules. Added a lockout mechanism for public users attempting to resume drafts. Now support using the routing service as a proxy for executing lookup rules in a DMZ configuration. Added the option to exclude team administrators and team managers in user task and email service task notifications. Now support the Retry function for user tasks in instances suspended when an error occurs after a submission but while a user task is still processing. Bug Fixes Report export pagination no longer cause duplicate results. (255941) Now able to download a report with many instances after upgrade from Forms 10.4.0. (306687) Scheduled reports follow the 'Sort Order as Descending' settings. (306689) Login with a windows account which is linked to a repository user works as expected. (315189) Users granted access to a process are able to perform user tasks for all forms. (316226) No longer received an LFF3602-UnsupportedSQLVersion when using Azure SQL databases. (316798) Can no longer save an invalid regular expression in a single_line successfully. (310630) Uncheck 'Use thousands delimiter' option for number/currency field works as expected. (304241) Current date/time default value correctly shows on newly added rows to a collection/table. (304254) Process error messages with certain types of errors can be displayed in the new form designer. (309774) The table/collection field can be dragged with a sub variable inside it to add new fields in the new form designer. (310628) Resources for the new form designer are updated in the browser. (304249) The URL for the comparison of designer features help page opens correctly. (310629) Related Links List of changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 update 1 Hotfix 1014310. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for the November 3, 2023 Laserfiche Windows and Android App
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for the November 3, 2023 Laserfiche Windows and Android App
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for the November 3, 2023 Laserfiche Windows and Android App Product: Laserfiche App KB: 1014494 Published: November 3, 2023 Last Updated: November 3, 2023 Summary This November 3, 2023 Android and Windows app release includes the following updates: Windows App Submitting offline forms containing attachments occasionally displayed a "Failed to read file for upload" error. This issue has been fixed. (371730) (429953) Signing out of the app and signing back into Forms now correctly displays the mobile view instead of the web view. (474320) Files can now be uploaded to classic forms when the Forms option to 'Prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery with a CSRF token' is enabled. (480930) Forms user tasks can now be re-assigned when the Forms option to 'Prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery with a CSRF token' is enabled. (482480) When signing into the app using LFDS and clicking a mobile link to a document, the app no longer gets stuck on the Laserfiche Server authentication page. (488122) Users now successfully receive a push notification for an assigned task when signed into a form in a different session. (488292) A file uploaded to a form as an attachment no longer disappears when upload is complete. (488332) A file uploaded to an online classic form can now be previewed successfully. (489803) Android App After signing in to the app and submitting a Forms task, signing out, signing back in, and attempting to submit another task sometimes displayed an error. This issue has been fixed. (488592) The app no longer occasionally closes unexpectedly when signing in. (490201) Notifications can now be successfully enabled for the Laserfiche app when using Android 13. (488590) The date entered into a date field of a draft or an offline form is now saved correctly. (423583) Fixed an issue on Android 12 and earlier devices where accessing the photo gallery displays a "Permission Denied" error. (494038) Windows and Android App When signing into the app using LFDS and clicking a mobile link to a document, the app no longer gets stuck on the Laserfiche Server authentication page. (488122) Users now successfully receive a push notification for an assigned task when signed into a form in a different session. (488292) A file uploaded to a form as an attachment no longer disappears when upload is complete. (488332) A file uploaded to an online classic form can now be previewed successfully. (489803) App Version Android: 2023.11.01.1238610 Windows: 2023.10.31.1237173 Resolution To update the Laserfiche app: Re-download the app from the Windows App Store or Google Play. Related Links 1014230 Laserfiche App Releases Laserfiche Android App 2023.11.01.1238610 APK File Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3 Product: Forms 11 KB: 1014414 Published: December 20, 2022 Last Updated: February 26, 2023 Summary Laserfiche introduces various new features with Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3 (11.0.2212.30907). For a more detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1014413. Laserfiche Forms Server System Requirements CPU: 4-core or more CPU Memory: 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended) Operating system: Windows 8.1 Windows 10 Windows 11 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2016 Windows Server 2019 Windows Server 2022 .Net Framework 4.8 must be installed. Note: Hardware requirements may fluctuate based on the number of users logged in to the server. If you expect to have many simultaneous connections to your Laserfiche Forms server, we encourage you to configure it with a faster CPU and/or add more RAM. Note: Server operating systems (Windows Server 2019, etc.) are preferred. Database Server Microsoft SQL Server 2014 SP3 or above Microsoft SQL Server 2016 SP2 or above Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Microsoft SQL Server 2019 Laserfiche Forms Software Requirements Laserfiche Server Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Server (version 9 or later) or Laserfiche Directory Server. Forms is not compatible with standalone editions of Laserfiche (Executive, Desktop, etc.). In order to integrate with Laserfiche Audit trail, Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Update 4 (or later) and Laserfiche Full Text Search 11.0 (or later). Web Server Internet Information Services (IIS): IIS 8.5 (Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 R2) IIS 10 (Windows 10, Windows 11, Windows Server 2016, Windows Server 2019, Windows Server 2022) ASP.NET 4.x must be enabled. Client Workstation Users can fill out forms that start a process in Chrome (latest version), Safari(Mac only), Firefox (latest version), Edge (Chromium based), Opera. The Laserfiche Forms Inbox, Form Designer, Process Designer, Administrative pages, etc. must be viewed in Edge (Chromium based), Firefox (latest version), or Chrome (latest version). Laserfiche Forms also supports mobile browsing (Chrome, Firefox, and Safari recommended). Known Issues Form designer: A lookup validation error occurs when previewing a form with lookup rules that use a deleted data source. (318332) Saving lookup rules fails when the selected data source is deleted. (318554) Browser freezes when using lookup values to add large numbers of new sets in a collection. (321714) The row count of a collection/table will not be correct if the 'append sets' choice is enabled when a lookup rule returns less data than the previous selection and there is another field with a value inside the collection/table. (324476) Put the previous/next button for pagination under the payment/reCAPTCHA section (368245) Improve accessibility for date picker used when fill form (370821) Cannot open instance detail page if the instance has more than 10k worker instances (395346) Forms Team JS Filter variable declaration error when test the filter/run the filter multiple times (397025) Need a way to clear the field value submitted in previous step and the field is hidden by field rule in later steps (403080) Update "Manage Access" of report schedule will trigger new snapshot generation if scheduled time is in the past. (306739) When importing a form from a current version of Forms to a previous version of Forms, the import process may not import features present in the current version of Forms. Forms only checks a user's group membership when creating their session token upon signing in. This means that removing a user from a group may not be reflected in a change in a user's Forms security until after the user signs out and signs back in. (168505) You may receive an InvalidDataSent error when you submit a form after waiting more than thirty minutes, while signed in as a Directory Server user. (134714) In certain situations, Forms may not properly process a field rule to ignore a value when payment processing is turned on. (125410) Forms may not fully synchronize some users that are members of a Directory Server group when using Laserfiche Server authentication. (74678) When dragging a variable generated by a formula from one form to another form, the formula is not transferred to the second form. (128416) On an iPad, the signature dialog offers only the option to draw a signature. Typing a signature is not possible on an iPad. (145050) Custom JavaScript "window onload = function setDate() {" displays when previewing a form but does not display when the form is published. To work around this known issue, try modifying the line with "function setDate() {" and then move the function call into a document ready function. (108444) SAML user accounts added with Directory Server synchronization through a SAML Group within a Directory Server group will be marked as invalid in the Named Users tab on the system Security page. Please also add the invalid SAML user

rows.title: Release Notes for Workflow 11 Update 3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Workflow 11 Update 3 Product: Workflow 11 KB: 1014462 Published: July 11, 2023 Last Updated: February 2, 2024 Summary Laserfiche Workflow 11.0.3 includes various feature improvements and bug fixes. For a detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1014461. Note: Workflow Web Designer has been retired and is no longer available. System Requirements Supported Operating Systems Windows 10 Windows 11 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2016 Windows Server 2019 Windows Server 2022 Note: 32-bit and 64-bit versions supported for all Workflow 10 and higher components. Note: .NET Framework 4.8 must be installed before installing Workflow. Requirements for the Workflow Designer Minimum: CPU 1GHz, 512MB RAM. Recommended: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM or higher. Requirements for the Workflow Server and Subscriber Minimum: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM. Recommended: 2, 4, 6, or 8-core CPU, based on need. 4GB RAM or higher. More CPU cores allow more workflows to run simultaneously. Software Requirements Windows Components Windows Message Queuing component: This component must be manually installed on the machine(s) hosting the Workflow Server and Subscriber. Microsoft .NET Framework 4.8: This component will automatically be installed during the Workflow installation if it is not installed. Windows Components Necessary for the Workflow Web Service IIS (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: You must install IIS 7 or higher. IIS Basic Authentication and Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. ASP.NET: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: If you are running Windows 8 or Windows Server 2012, you must install the ASP.NET 4.5 feature in IIS. On Windows 10 or Windows Server 2016, the feature may be listed as ASP.NET 4.6 or 4.7. Supported Database Management Systems SQL Server 2014 SP3 SQL Server 2016 SQL Server 2017 SQL Server 2019 SQL Server 2022 Note: Express editions of the SQL Server versions listed above are also supported but not recommended for high-volume Workflow installations. Note: Oracle DBMS are no longer supported. If you are upgrading from a previous version of Workflow, installation of SQL Server will be required. Laserfiche Components Laserfiche Server 10 or higher (Laserfiche Server 10.4.3 or higher is recommended.) Component Known Issue Workflow Whitepapers The whitepapers "Building Custom Activities in Workflow" for both C# and VB contain examples using Visual Studio 2010 and .NET Framework 4. Workflow 11 and newer require Visual Studio 2019 and .NET Framework 4.8 or newer. Workflow Installation Windows 11 does not support Laserfiche Workflow Server. All other components of Workflow can be installed in Windows 11, but the server component will need to be on another machine. Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, you may need to provide domain authentication instead of using Local System Account when configuring the Workflow Subscriber. (77933) Workflow Installation When using the Repair option to restore assemblies that were deleted from the GAC, the process may fail. Repairing it a second time will succeed. (70389) Workflow Installation When upgrading to Workflow 9 or later, if the Workflow 8.3 uninstall process fails, it must be manually uninstalled by running the original installer. (95866) Workflow Installation Uninstalling Workflow does not deactivate your license. Prior to uninstalling, you must delete the license in the Workflow Administration Console to deactivate it. Workflow Server/Windows Firewall If the Workflow Server is installed on a different machine than the Laserfiche Server, the Windows Firewall will block the Workflow Server when a business process is launched from a Laserfiche client application that is using a non-default port. (90610) Workflow Server If you create an entry with the same name as an existing entry and if both entries are inside the same folder, the created entry will be automatically renamed--even if the created entry is of a different type (folder or document) than the existing entry. (65791) Workflow Server The Configuration Manager can be slow to recognize the Workflow Server's configuration status after manually stopping and restarting the service. Wait a few seconds and press F5 to have the Configuration Manager display the correct status. (99659) Workflow Server When testing a database activity's connection, if the database driver crashes, the Workflow Server will also crash, even if the Workflow Server is configured to run the database activities in an external process. (99409) Workflow Database A DateDiff overflow error can occur when opening the Activities view. Use the Activities2 view instead. (98609) Workflow Subscriber The Che

Error Messages: Take note of any error messages that occurred. Please be precise in getting the exact text of the .... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013987/general-technical-support-checkli ...
rows.title: General Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche 10 and Higher
rows.detailTitle: General Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche 10 and Higher
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith General Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche 10 and Higher Product: Laserfiche KB: 1013987 Published: August 23, 2018 Last Updated: April 16, 2024 Summary There are certain pieces of information that are regularly requested for most support cases, regardless of the specific Laserfiche product you are using. Having this information ready can expedite the handling of your support case. For solution providers opening a case from the support site, please have this information beforehand so that when you open the case, you can supply the information at that time as well. Commonly requested information and files Software version and build numbers: Take note of the exact version number and build number of the Laserfiche application which is experiencing issues, whether it is the Laserfiche Server, client, or other Laserfiche program. Most applications will have a "Help - About" menu item that has version information. The Laserfiche Server's version and build can be found by selecting the server name in the Laserfiche Administration Console. Error Messages: Take note of any error messages that occurred. Please be precise in getting the exact text of the error message. Screenshots and timestamps will be very useful. How to reproduce the error: Provide a description of any steps you took leading up to the error. It may be useful to take a video recording of the steps. Files needed to reproduce the error: Please provide sample material necessary to reproduce the issue. For example: workflow, business process, sample documents, or a briefcase of repository objects. Scale and scope: Specify whether the problem is computer-specific or happens on multiple locations. For instance, it is important to note whether the problem is specific to a particular computer, user, group, volume, template, repository, document, or page. If the issue is more widespread, take note whether it occurs in all situations or without a discernible pattern. Changes made prior: Provide a description of any changes to the system or program that were made prior to the error occurring. Event Viewer logs: Save copies of the Application and System Event Logs from both the Laserfiche Server computer and other affected servers and workstations in their native .evtx or .evt formats. Open the Event Viewer from Administrative Tools. Expand the Windows Logs item. Right-click on Application and click Save All Events As. Specify a name and click Save. Repeat with the System item. For Laserfiche Server specific log data, open the Applications and Services Logs node, expand Laserfiche, ContentRepository, Service and view the Admin and Operational logs. Troubleshooting so far: Indicate exactly what troubleshooting steps have been done so far. This will help expedite the troubleshooting process by avoiding suggestions that have already been performed. Operating system and service pack: Take note of the operating system and service packs in use on the computer or computers in question. If the problem is with a Laserfiche program installed on a computer other than the Laserfiche server, it is useful to note the Operating system and service pack on both the server computer and on the computer in question. Database Management System: Take note of the type of database management system in use by Laserfiche (Microsoft SQL Server Express, Microsoft SQL Server), as well as its full version and where it is located in relation to the Laserfiche Server. Additional Tasks Upgrade: If it is possible to upgrade to the latest version of Laserfiche Server and the client program you are using, please try to do so. In many situations, it will lead to a resolution. Product Checklists 1014509 Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche Cloud 1012411 Technical Support Checklist for Product Activation and Deactivation. 1013990 Laserfiche Scanning 10 and Higher Technical Support Checklist 1013993 Quick Fields 10 and Higher Technical Support Checklist. 1013988 Web Products 10 and Higher Technical Support Checklist. 1013989 Workflow 10 and Higher Technical Support Checklist. 1014485 Forms 10 and Higher Technical Support Checklist. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

(316798) See List of Changes For Laserfiche Forms 11 Hotfix 1014297 for a fix to this issue. When importing a form from a current version of Forms to a previous version of Forms, the import process may not import features present in the current version of Forms. Forms only checks a user's group membership when creating their session token upon signing in. This means that removing a user from a group may not be reflected in a change in a user's Forms security until after the user signs out and signs back in. (168505) You may receive an InvalidDataSent error when you submit a form after waiting more than thirty minutes, while signed in as a Directory Server user. (134714) In certain situations, Forms may not properly process a field rule to ignore a value when payment processing is turned on. (125410) Forms may not fully synchronize some users that are members of a Directory Server group when using Laserfiche Server authentication. (74678) When dragging a variable generated by a formula from one form to another form, the formula is not transferred to the second form. (128416) On an iPad, the signature dialog offers only the option to draw a signature. Typing a signature is not possible on an iPad. (145050) Custom JavaScript "window onload = function setDate() {" displays when previewing a form but does not display when the form is published. To work around this known issue, try modifying the line with "function setDate() {" and then move the function call into a document ready function. (108444) SAML user accounts added with Directory Server synchronization through a SAML Group within a Directory Server group will be marked as invalid in the Named Users tab on the system Security page. Please also add the invalid SAML user accounts to a Directory Server group that is authorized to sign in to Forms. (111872) In Arabic (Saudi Arabia), scientific notation from a lookup in a number field is displayed in decimal format instead of comma format. (60404) In Arabic (Saudi Arabia), read-only values from a formula in a thank you message are displayed in decimal format instead of comma format. (60405) When you are using the inbox and then change HTTP to HTTPS in Forms Configuration, you must refresh the page to perform an inbox task. (60667) After you generate a report and then change the report type, the downloaded report will have the original report type, while the saved snapshot will have the new report type. (61162) In Forms 10.3, the height of a single-line row in a collection is 46 pixels on the submit page and 45 pixels on the submitted page. In Forms 10.3.1 and later, the two are 46 pixels. (57759) In Forms 10.3, the "Other" value in a checkbox on the submit page is tagged with "nput", while in Forms 10.3.1 and later, it is tagged with "textarea." (63793) Commas are converted to periods when you have a number value from a lookup in a text column that updates a number field. (62276) In all versions of Internet Explorer, you cannot view an uploaded image on a form. (60963) In Firefox, you cannot CTRL +

rows.title: While Installing Laserfiche Forms, You May Receive the Following Error Message: "Error 1923. Service 'Laserfiche Forms Routing Service' (Laserfiche Forms Routing Service) could not be installed."
rows.detailTitle: While Installing Laserfiche Forms, You May Receive the Following Error Message: "Error 1923. Service 'Laserfiche Forms Routing Service' (Laserfiche Forms Routing Service) could not be installed."
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in While Installing Laserfiche Forms, You May Receive the Following Error Message: "Error 1923. Service 'Laserfiche Forms Routing Service' (Laserfiche Forms Routing Service) could not be installed." Product: Product KB: 1014066 Published: March 17, 2020 Last Updated: March 17, 2020 Summary While installing Laserfiche Forms, you may receive the following error: "Error 1923. Service ''Laserfiche Forms Routing Service'' (Laserfiche Forms Routing Service) could not be installed. Verify that you have sufficient privileges to install system services." Cause You may have specified an invalid service user for the Laserfiche Forms Routing Service. Resolution Ensure that you have a valid user account for the Laserfiche Forms Routing Service. For example, if you are using a domain account, make sure to include the domain as part of the username (e.g., domain\username). 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for the January 31, 2025 Laserfiche App Product: Laserfiche App KB: 1014555 Published: January 31, 2025 Last Updated: February 3, 2025 Summary This January 31, 2025 app release includes the following updates: iOS and Android When submitting an offline form, an error is no longer displayed when the file upload field is collapsed or pagination is used. rows.snippet: Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 5 years ago https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013897/release-notes-for-laserfiche-connector-10-3 Laserfiche Connector Summary Laserfiche Connector provides a streamlined experience for integ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013897/release-notes-for-laserfiche-c ...
rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Product: Laserfiche Connector KB: 1013897 Published: November 15, 2017 Last Updated: October 27, 2020 Summary Laserfiche Connector provides a streamlined experience for integrating Laserfiche with line of business applications such as CRM and ERP systems. Laserfiche Connector 10.3 is a major release that includes options to start workflows and Workflow business processes, the option to run a profile from a command line argument, the ability to edit profiles with the Profile Creation wizard, an updated interface, and more. For a detailed list of changes, please see 1013896 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3. Laserfiche Connector 10.3 is currently available as a reseller-only pre-release. Service Pack and Hotfix Information 1014233 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 5 1014194 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 4 1014126 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 3 1014068 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 2 1013958 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 1 System and Software Requirements System CPU: 2.93GHz or faster processor Memory: 4GB Operating system: Windows Server 2008 (SP 2+) and later, or Windows Vista (SP2+) and later Software .NET 4.5 Microsoft Visual C++ 2012 Redistributable (x64) -11.0.61030 Microsoft Visual C++ 2012 Redistributable (x86) -11.0.61030 Microsoft Visual C++ 2017 Redistributable (x64) -14.11.25325 Microsoft Visual C++ 2017 Redistributable (x86) -14.11.25325 Laserfiche server version 9.0 or later Laserfiche Web Access version 8.3 or later Laserfiche web client version 10.2 or later Laserfiche WebLink 8.0 or later Laserfiche Scanning 9.0 or later Laserfiche Workflow 10.0 or later To integrate Laserfiche Connector with a web application Internet Explorer: versions 9, 10, or 11 Firefox: the latest version (auto-updated) Chrome: the latest version (auto-updated) Microsoft Edge To integrate Laserfiche Connector with a Java Application on Windows system Java Runtime Environment (JRE): versions 6 update 21, 7, or 8 Java Development Kit (JDK): versions 6 update 21, 7, or 8 Java Access Bridge (JAB): version 2.0.2 or later enabled Testing the Laserfiche Connector before installing it The Laserfiche Connector Test Utility lets you quickly determine if Laserfiche Connector will work with an application. Download and run the Test Utility here. System and Software requirements, as well as instructions, can be found in the Laserfiche Connector help. Unattended batch installation The fastest way to distribute Laserfiche Connector and the Laserfiche Connector profiles you've created to multiple machines is to perform a command line or unattended installation and store XML profiles on a network drive all. Learn more about unattended installs. Third-party software accessibility Some additional configuration may be necessary to run Laserfiche Connector from Internet Explorer, an application that hosts Internet Explorer controls, Chrome, Firefox, or a Java-based application. See these instructions for more information. See also the list of applications Laserfiche has confirmed compatibility with. Known Issues You may receive a "there is no document currently open" error when using the "Start workflow" or "Start Workflow business process" actions with the "Currently open Laserfiche document" option if Connector is launched as an administrator, but the Laserfiche client is not. (44788) Connector 10.3 does not support editing Connector 10.2 profiles that use the "Write data to an application" action. (52459) When using Laserfiche Connector on a remote desktop, and when using OCR to analyze a window, you may get unexpected results if the target application is partially hidden by another application. Ensure the application you are targeting does not have any windows or applications showing in front of it. (128129) If the control you are getting information from has any of the following characters in its ID: " !"#$%&'()*+,./:;<=>?@[\]^`{|}~", the profile will not run correctly. Escape these characters by adding \\ (two backslashes) before them in the profile's XML. (30690, 153713, 146019) A profile run from the command line will silently fail if Laserfiche Connector is run as an administrator and the command is not run as an administrator. (50636) The %(Count) token is not resolved when a Laserfiche Connector profile runs. (140853) When selecting a control in Chrome with UI automation, you cannot select controls hidden behind a scroll bar inside an iFrame that has a different source. (120818) If you change text size through Personalize -> Display in Windows, Laserfiche Connector selection rectangles may be offset in certain applications. To get the rectangles to be position correctly, open the properties for the target application and select "Disable display scaling on high DPI settings" on the Compatibility tab. After you've configured Laserfich

rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 4
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 4 Product: Laserfiche Connector 10.3 KB: 1014194 Published: August 10, 2020 Last Updated: October 27, 2020 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 4. See the following list of changes: Connector now properly detects page elements in recent versions of Firefox. (229481) You no longer receive a "Could not connect to the Firefox Remote Debugger" error message when Connector attempts to analyze a web page in current versions of Firefox. (261330) This update includes the following files: LFC.exe (10.3.0.332) LFCUIAComWrapper.dll (10.3.0.332) LFC.resources.dll (10.3.0.332) LFC.exe.config (Last modified 2/20/2020) analyzeWebpageLayout.js (Last modified 2/20/2020) findElement.js (Last modified 8/8/2019) findElementForFF.js (Last modified 8/8/2019) Resolution To apply the update: Click the following link to download a zip file containing a self-extracting EXE file containing Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 4. KB1014194.zip Extract the contents of the zip file to a location on the computer hosting Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Double-click LFConnector_1030332_KB1014194.exe to update Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 4. Related Links 1014126 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 3 1014068 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 2 1013958 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Update 1 1013896 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 1013897 Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 10.3 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
